violations.text: Drinks were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler & retail area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2025

Single-use articles were observed stored on the floor in the storage area near the bathroom. Single-service articles must be stored at least 6 inches off the ground. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2025

Establishment did not have a person in charge that has successfully completed an approved food safety managers certification course. Please email inspector proof that additional employees have been enrolled and completed an approved food safety course. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024

Establishment did not have a person in charge that has successfully completed an approved food safety certification course. Please email inspector proof that additional employees have been enrolled and completed an approved food safety course. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024

The ware-washing area does not have a hand-washing sink that is conveniently located and/or readily accessible. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as bottled beverages) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as bottled beverages during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024

Milk, Half & Half, cream, and light cream were observed held at 48-54 degrees Fahrenheit on ice packs on the coffee station counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been on the ice packs for about 3.5 hours prior to inspection. Food items were relocated to the walk-in cooler. Establishment must not cold-hold products on ice packs. Per person in charge, food items will be stored under refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024

Self-service food items (donuts, turnovers, and twists) in the food establishment are lacking label information. Self-service food items in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Food items were removed from the shelf and must not be sold until the required label information is available to the customer. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024

Single-use articles were observed stored on the floor in the storage area near the bathroom. Single-service articles must be stored at least 6 inches off the ground. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024
